text.fullText: Brokers for the trucking industry should be regulated in what they pay and the amount of commission they are earning from each load hauled by drivers, and transparent with what they are paid by the shippers and given to the carriers for their information. It also seems the brokers want to have total control on everything in the trucking industry, they should not be allowed to monitor anything, The ELD or have any access to any device that has to do with the operation of a truck, they do not own the truck or pay the required insurance, pay for fuel, or perform any maintenance nor do they pay the driver. The brokers are only the middleman between the shipper and receiver, which regulations should be required for the shipper and receiver the same as the brokers. Trucking is a stressful job not to be taken lightly, the driver has enough regulations to follow with the fmcsa and those are stressful enough, there needs to be transparency in everything the brokers, shipper and receivers. Tracking loads is another issue that is very annoying as in some instances tracking does not work on drivers phones, and drivers are required by the brokers to have tracking at all times during shipment, who pays for this? It's stated in their contract that drivers and companies are required to have tracking on at all times. And the fleetguard issue is above the threshold, brokers are not God's of the trucking industry and they need to be investigated in their untruthful tactics and be a better reputable company than trying to get rich off the backs of trucking companies who struggle with just barely earning 2.25 a mile to operate a truck to move freight with that figure should always be the the first to consider before any other negotiation takes place. Transparency is not the only issue it's what's hidden beneath that transparency they don't want us to know about, it's shameful that a company can earn or abuse trucking companies to profit billions of dollars off the backs of companies and drivers and never considering the outcome of the real problem at hand, which I would call theft but I can't make that assumption. The profits of brokers should be brought before Congress and everything they have expenditures for should be transparent to the companies who rely on brokers for their loads, there used to be load boards in the 80s and 90s at truck stops before brokers took over and stopped the load boards as they were all taken down so the transparency is hidden with the brokers, drivers knew how much the load paid on the load boards but now it's your guess is as good as mine. Break this cycle of broker abuse and start making rules and regulations for these criminals, again they do not control the trucking industry, we the drivers are the ones doing the labor to get freight where it needs to go for any and all necessary product going to the public or companies, the risk is higher in the trucking industry because of new people coming in every day and that needs to be addressed as well, our safety is becoming dangerous instead of safety being safe.
